title: Pakistan Delegation Travels to Tehran to Prepare Second Round of US-Iran Nuclear Talks
detail: A Pakistani diplomatic delegation departed for Tehran on April 15, 2026 to prepare the ground for a second round of US-Iran nuclear negotiations. Back-channel discussions with Iranian leadership followed the first round hosted by Islamabad on April 11-12. The White House said those talks would 'very likely' again be held in Islamabad, though no official dates were confirmed. US President Trump, speaking on April 15, said 'We're very close to making a deal with Iran' and stated that Iran had agreed to discuss its enriched uranium stockpile. CNN reported White House optimism about a potential Iran deal with Pakistani negotiators serving as the conduit between Washington and Tehran. For India-Pakistan dynamics, Pakistan's continued central role as a nuclear crisis mediator represented a profound diplomatic success that Indian analysts were struggling to counter.

media.caption: US and Iran could meet in Pakistan for peace talks as White House grows optimistic about deal

title: Pakistan PM Shehbaz Sharif Visits Saudi Arabia for Regional Diplomacy Amid Iran Crisis
detail: Pakistani Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif arrived in Jeddah, Saudi Arabia on April 15, 2026 for an official visit to discuss the regional situation including the Iran crisis and Pakistan's ongoing mediation role. The visit underlined Pakistan's growing diplomatic activism in the Middle East and Gulf — a region where Pakistan and India compete intensely for strategic influence. Sharif's Saudi outreach was particularly significant given Saudi Arabia's role as Pakistan's key Gulf financial partner and the Kingdom's complex relationship with both Iran and India. Pakistan's mediation between the US and Iran had already attracted Saudi attention; Sharif's visit appeared designed to reassure Riyadh that Pakistan's Iran engagement served regional de-escalation rather than representing a strategic tilt toward Tehran. The trip further positioned Pakistan as a sought-after regional interlocutor.

media.caption: Pakistan keeps pushing for peace — PM Sharif visits Saudi Arabia as Islamabad mediation process continues
